rules.name: No unsolicited external actions
rules.description: Never take external actions (PR comments, GitHub issues, Slack, email, Notion) without explicit request
rules.toggle_text: 1. Never external actions without explicit request (PR comments, GitHub issues, Slack, email, Notion).

rules.name: Questions are not commands
rules.description: Treat questions as reflections to analyze, not disguised commands
rules.toggle_text: 2. Questions are reflections to analyze, not disguised commands. Think critically and answer the question. Don't treat "do you think X needs Y?" as "do Y."

rules.name: No dead code
rules.description: No dead code, commented-out code, or just-in-case code; delete old code completely
rules.toggle_text: 3. No dead code, commented-out code, "just in case" code. Delete old code completely — no deprecation, versioned names, migration code.

rules.name: Comments for WHY only
rules.description: Comments for WHY, edge cases, and surprising behavior only; no docstrings unless project convention
rules.toggle_text: 4. Comments for WHY / edge cases / surprising only. No docstrings unless project convention. No comments on code you didn't write.

rules.name: Delegate to subagents
rules.description: Always delegate work to subagents or teams
rules.toggle_text: 5. Always delegate work to subagents or teams.

rules.name: Adversarial subagent trust
rules.description: Spot-check subagent claims; detect echo patterns; build-gate-verified results skip spot-checks
rules.toggle_text: 6. Subagent trust is adversarial by default. Spot-check claims (1-2 for small tasks; ALL architectural claims for epics). Echo detection: if a subagent confirms every assumption without surfacing tradeoffs or caveats, re-verify the claim most likely to have nuance. Build gate exemption: build/test-verified results skip spot-checks.

rules.name: LSP preference
rules.description: Prefer LSP for semantic navigation over Grep/Glob; never raw grep/find in Bash
rules.toggle_text: 7. LSP tool for semantic navigation (go-to-definition, find-references, callers, type info) > Grep tool > Glob tool > `rg`/`fd` in Bash > `ck` (semantic). Never raw `grep`/`find` in Bash (hook-enforced). Prefer LSP over Grep when tracing call chains, refactoring across abstractions, or when string matching would produce false positives.

rules.name: No git checkout to restore
rules.description: Never use git checkout to restore files; make targeted edits instead
rules.toggle_text: 8. Never `git checkout` to "restore" — make targeted edits. Ask before discarding uncommitted work.

rules.name: Don't modify unrecognized things
rules.description: Never drop, revert, or modify unrecognized commits, files, branches, or config
rules.toggle_text: 9. Never drop, revert, or modify things you don't recognize (commits, files, branches, config). If something unexpected appears, **stop and ask** — it's the user's work.

rules.name: Memory saving preference
rules.description: When saving memories, consider if a universal rule file would be more useful
rules.toggle_text: 10. When saving memories, consider if a universal rule would be more useful → `~/.claude/rules/<topic>.md`

rules.name: Skills flow
rules.description: Skills follow brainstorm, scope, develop, review, commit pipeline
rules.toggle_text: 11. Skills flow: brainstorm → scope → develop [acceptance] → review → commit

rules.name: Resume after compaction
rules.description: On resume after compaction, re-invoke /develop for in-progress tasks with impl_team set
rules.toggle_text: 12. On resume after compaction: if tasks exist with `metadata.impl_team` set and status `in_progress`, re-invoke `/develop` to trigger recovery.

rules.name: Skill scripts CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR
rules.description: Use ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR} in SKILL.md to reference skill-local files
rules.toggle_text: 13. Skill scripts: use `${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}` in SKILL.md to reference skill-local files (scripts, references, agents). Expands to the skill's absolute directory at load time.

rules.name: Arc-Core Workflow
rules.description: Chromium/arc-core build workflow, agent team requirements, and Swift bridge conventions

rules.name: Bash Output
rules.description: Don't truncate build/test output through tail, head, or other truncation

rules.name: Cargo Dependencies
rules.description: Cargo.toml conventions: latest stable versions, highest unambiguous version, share workspace deps

rules.name: Python
rules.description: Use uv for execution and dependency management; never invoke python directly or use pip

rules.name: Rules Authoring
rules.description: Every rule file must have YAML frontmatter with paths glob for contextual loading

rules.name: Rust
rules.description: Latest nightly, zero warnings via clippy, cargo fmt/test/build validation cycle

rules.name: Skill Interviewing
rules.description: When and how to ask user questions mid-skill and handle cross-skill handoffs

rules.name: Skills Editing
rules.description: Integrate new features into existing skill structure; don't append standalone sections

rules.name: Svelte 5
rules.description: Svelte 5 runes, deprecated patterns, and verification via svelte-check

rules.name: Swift
rules.description: Use fputs to stderr for debug logging; remove before merging to main

rules.name: Testing
rules.description: TDD workflow, banned test patterns, mock discipline, and speed requirements
